Here are all the hardcoded email addresses found in the webapp source code:

| # | File | Line | Email Address |
|---|------|------|---------------|
| 1 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 9 | `alice@example.com` |
| 2 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 10 | `bob@example.com` |
| 3 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 11 | `charlie@example.com` |

**All three are in `UsersController.scala`**, embedded directly in what appears to be a hardcoded in-memory user list (seeded test/dummy data):

```scala
User(1, "Alice",   "alice@example.com",   LocalDateTime.of(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30)),
User(2, "Bob",     "bob@example.com",     LocalDateTime.of(2024, 2, 20, 14,  0)),
User(3, "Charlie", "charlie@example.com", LocalDateTime.of(2024, 3,  5,  9, 15)),
```

**Recommendation:** These hardcoded user records (including emails) should be moved out of source code and into a database, a seeding script, or at minimum an external configuration/fixture file — especially before any production deployment.
